title: physematium oreganum subsp. cathcartianum
intro: Physematium oreganum subsp. cathcartianum, also known as the Tibetan cordyceps or caterpillar fungus, is a type of fungus that infects the larvae of certain insects and grows into a unique, above-ground stem. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Tibetan and Chinese cultures, for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Physematium oreganum subsp. cathcartianum are said to include improved lung function, increased energy, and enhanced immune system function, as well as potential anti-aging effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and processing the fungus, and are often consumed as a supplement in capsule or powder form.
